title: Le perfecto
summary: Say what you've done: the perfect tense (ha + past participle).

teach.note: -ar verbs form the past participle with -ate: parlar → parlate.
-er/-ir verbs use -ite: vider → vidite, audir → audite.
The perfect tense: ha (have) + past participle, same pattern as English.

grammar.title: The perfect tense
grammar.body:
Interlingua's perfect tense works just like English "have done": the auxiliary **ha** plus a past participle.
Form the past participle from the infinitive: **-ar → -ate**, **-er/-ir → -ite**.
parlar → parlate, mangiar → mangiate, vider → vidite, audir → audite.
One form for every person: io ha parlate, tu ha parlate, nos ha parlate — the auxiliary never changes.

teach.note: apprender is -er, so its participle is apprendite.
